summary: SABIC sought July PE bids: LDPE Film at USD 1,120/ton CIF, LLDPE Film at USD 970/ton CIF, and HDPE Film at USD 1,010/ton CIF.
PetroChina LLDPE Film 7042N fell another USD 20/ton to USD 940/ton CIF; STL HDPE Film FS7000 stayed at USD 1,070/ton CIF.
Domestic LLDPE spot offers were mainly VND 30,000-30,500/kg, while HDPE blow offers dropped sharply to VND 29,000-31,500/kg.
paras: Import offers and bid levels

A Saudi SABIC distributor sought new July PE bids from Vietnam buyers. Indicative bid levels were: LDPE Film down to USD 1,120/ton CIF, LDPE coating at USD 1,360/ton CIF, LLDPE Film at USD 970/ton CIF, LLDPE injection at USD 1,100/ton CIF, and HDPE Film at USD 1,010/ton CIF.
SABIC was targeting LDPE Film closing prices from around USD 1,070/ton CIF, but buyer bids at this level were limited.
Lotte Hyundai Korea offered LDPE Film LF410, LF512 and LF311 to Vietnam at USD 1,100/ton CIF. Most buyers were only bidding around USD 1,000-1,040/ton CIF, well below the offers.

Petlin Malaysia returned to the Vietnam LDPE market on 7/7. LDPE Film C150Y was offered at USD 1,200/ton CIF, with bids accepted from USD 1,180/ton CIF. Petlin's 255,000-ton/year LDPE plant restarted in June after around 50 days of major maintenance, but the offer was still considered high versus the market level and attracted limited negotiation interest.
Another major Malaysian producer, Lotte Titan, is expected to offer August PE cargoes to Vietnam later this week.

For China-origin cargoes, PetroChina LLDPE Film 7042N (MI 2, with additive) was offered to Vietnam at USD 940/ton CIF, down another USD 20/ton from last week. PetroChina LLDPE 7046 (MI 1) was offered at USD 950/ton CIF, while STL kept HDPE Film FS7000 unchanged at USD 1,070/ton CIF.
Domestic LDPE and LLDPE market

Domestic LDPE offers showed a wide supplier spread. A leading resin trading company offered Lotte Titan Malaysia LDPE Film 260GG at VND 41,500/kg, VAT included, while Lotte Titan LDPE 200GG was offered at VND 38,500/kg, VAT included. The two grades therefore showed a gap of up to VND 3,000/kg despite having similar import offer levels.
Some US- and China-origin LDPE Film offers were at a lower level of around VND 38,000/kg, VAT included.

For LLDPE, several traders reduced offers for Qamar FD21HS and SABIC 218WJ to VND 27,000-27,500/kg, VAT included, for far-month September 2026 delivery. However, spot cargo offers were still mainly at VND 30,000-30,500/kg, VAT included.

Sellers tried to hold prices to avoid losses on cargoes booked when prices spiked in March-April, while buyers continued to purchase cautiously. The cheapest recorded LLDPE was China-origin 7042/7050, with July-delivery transactions at VND 28,700-29,000/kg, VAT included.
Domestic HDPE market

HDPE F1 of Qamar, TR144 of Marlex and FI00952 of SABIC Saudi Arabia were commonly offered at VND 35,500-36,000/kg, VAT included, flat to down VND 500/kg from late last week. These HDPE Film grades maintained a relatively high price level because immediate domestic supply was not abundant.

HDPE blow prices dropped sharply. On 7/7, an LSP distributor offered CP Chem Singapore HDPE blow 5502 at VND 31,500/kg, VAT included. Other traders lowered China- and US-origin HDPE blow offers to VND 29,000-30,000/kg, VAT included, down heavily by VND 2,000-3,000/kg from late last week.

summary: PetroChina PP yarn L5E89 rebounded slightly to USD 1,010/ton CIF after a USD 85/ton cut to USD 1,000/ton CIF late last week.
PetroChina BOPP was offered at USD 1,015/ton CIF; Lotte Korea PP yarn Y130 was offered at USD 1,000/ton CIF.
BSR PP T3034 in southern Vietnam fell to VND 29,800/kg, becoming the lowest-priced domestic PP yarn product.
paras: Import offers

Late last week, a distributor cut PetroChina PP yarn L5E89 offers to Vietnam sharply to USD 1,000/ton CIF, down USD 85/ton from the previous week. This week, the offer edged up by USD 10/ton to USD 1,010/ton CIF, but closing prices may remain unchanged.
PetroChina BOPP was offered to Vietnam on 7/7 at USD 1,015/ton CIF.
A Lotte Korea distributor offered Lotte PP yarn Y130 to Vietnam at USD 1,000/ton CIF on 7/7.

Previous low-priced PP cargoes from Hanwha Korea, including PP homo at USD 990/ton CIF and PP copo at USD 1,020/ton CIF, had been sold out. Current China- and Korea-origin PP homo offers to Vietnam are now quite similar and among the cheapest in the market, with the downward trend pausing at the beginning of the week.
Domestic market
In southern Vietnam, BSR PP T3034 selling prices were recorded falling sharply to VND 29,800/kg, VAT included, becoming the lowest-priced domestic PP yarn product at this point.
In northern Vietnam, the PP market had not recorded major early-week movement. Actual transactions ranged from stable to slightly lower, but the decline slowed compared with last week.
For NSRP PP 114G/105G, the lowest offer remained at VND 30,500/kg, VAT included, flat from late last week, while actual transaction prices may reach around VND 30,000/kg, VAT included.
PP copo offers were also mostly unchanged. However, for off-grade PP copo, Stavian offered some SK Chemical Korea-origin cargoes at VND 38,500/kg, VAT included, down VND 500/kg from late last week.

summary: Northern China-origin coal-based PVC SG-5 offers were broadly aligned at VND 20,200-20,400/kg.
The coal-based PVC floor stayed at VND 20,200/kg, but transactions were very slow as buyers expected further declines.
Asian VCM feedstock prices fell another USD 20-25/ton, adding pressure to oil-based PVC prices.
paras: Domestic PVC SG-5 market
Based on the 7/7 market survey, northern China-origin coal-based PVC SG-5 offers from suppliers were broadly aligned at VND 20,200-20,400/kg, VAT included.
Examples in the north included Yihua PVC SG-5 at VND 20,400/kg, Zhongtai PVC SG-5 at VND 20,300/kg, and Yanhu/Xinzhonggu PVC SG-5 at VND 20,200/kg, VAT included.

The floor price for coal-based PVC remained at VND 20,200/kg, VAT included, unchanged from late June, indicating that suppliers are trying to protect margins. However, transactions remained very slow because most buyers were not rushing to replenish stock and expected further price declines.
Many market participants expected coal-based PVC prices could fall to around VND 19,000/kg, VAT included, during July.
In southern Vietnam, a key Yihua distributor offered Yihua PVC SG-5 at VND 20,100/kg, VAT included, cheaper than northern offers.
Oil-based PVC and feedstock pressure

In the south, leading distributor Stavian offered Shintech US PVC SE950/SE1000 at VND 20,800/kg, VAT included, down VND 200-300/kg from last week to reduce inventory. However, most buyers only bid below VND 20,500/kg, VAT included.
Domestic producer grades such as PM66R and SG660/SP600 were commonly offered by traders at VND 20,900-21,200/kg, VAT included, lower than the factory offers released in late June.
Market sources reported that Asian VCM feedstock prices fell another USD 20-25/ton this week versus last week, adding further downward pressure on oil-based PVC prices.
Note: All prices are reported as shown in the 7/7 market update; VND prices include VAT unless stated otherwise.

editorial.changedNote: 15 tracked average products moved from Jul 6; PP copo recorded the largest group drop.
